meta.title: CAVE2-Score
meta.cff_info:
CAVE2 Score

Huang et al., 2023

DOI: 10.1186/s12883023-03510-1

Factors and Points

  • Cortex involvement:
    • Yes: 2 points
  • Age:
    • <65 years: 1 point
  • Volume:
    • >10 mL: 1 point
  • Seizures:
    • Early seizures within 7 days of ICH: 1 point

Risk by CAVE2 Score

After 4 years:

  • 0 points: 4.5% risk
  • 1 point: 5.7% risk
  • 2 points: 18.3% risk
  • 3 points: 16.1% risk
  • 4 points: 42.4% risk
  • 5 points: 75% risk

Comparison with Original CAVE Score

Refer to Table 3 in the publication for a detailed comparison between the CAVE and CAVE2 scores and their respective relationships to late seizures.

Clinical Utility

The CAVE2 score refines the original CAVE score by introducing adjustments to the scoring system (e.g., doubling the weight of cortical involvement) and improves predictive accuracy for late seizures following intracerebral hemorrhage (ICH). This score is particularly useful for identifying patients who may benefit from closer follow-up or preventive strategies.

Additional Study Information

Category Details
Score Name CAVE2 Score
Population Derivation: 408 patients, Taiwan (2013-2019)
Statistical Methods Binary logistic regression
Validation Methods Internal: Retrospective cohort (Ditmanson Medical Foundation Chiayi Christian Hospital)
Performance Metrics c-statistic: 0.74
Risk scores: 4.6% (≤1 point), 18.3% (2 points), 54.1% (≥3 points)
Generalizability Moderate (limited by single-center study)
Clinical Utility Improved accuracy compared to the original CAVE score. Useful for identifying high-risk patients and guiding follow-up strategies.
meta.authors: Huang Y, Wong Y, Wu C, et al.
meta.clinical_utility: Background and purpose Seizures commonly occur in patients with intracerebral hemorrhage (ICH). Anticonvulsants are commonly used for preventing seizures in patients with ICH.
meta.outcome: Modified CAVE score for predicting late seizures after intracerebral hemorrhage

predictors.name: Cortical Involvement
predictors.assess: Indicates whether the intracerebral hemorrhage (ICH) involves the cortex. Cortical involvement is associated with a significantly increased risk of late seizures. Odds Ratio (OR): 3.19 (95% CI: 2.09–4.87).

predictors.name: Age
predictors.assess: Refers to the age of the patient at the time of ICH. Younger patients have higher risks of late seizures. Odds Ratio (OR): 1.73 (95% CI: 1.14–2.64).

predictors.name: Volume
predictors.assess: Refers to the volume of the intracerebral hemorrhage (ICH). In the CAVE2 Score, volume was calculated using the ABC/2 method based on CT imaging. Calculation Method (ABC/2): A: Maximum diameter of the hemorrhage on the largest slice (in cm). B: Diameter perpendicular to "A" on the same slice (in cm). C: Approximate number of slices with visible hemorrhage, multiplied by the slice thickness (in cm). Formula: Volume = A × B × C / 2​. Odds Ratio (OR): 1.67 (95% CI: 1.1–2.55) for volumes >10 mL.

predictors.name: Acute Symptomatic Seizures/ Early Seizures
predictors.assess: Refers to seizures occurring within the first 7 days of ICH. Early seizures are a strong predictor of late seizures. Odds Ratio (OR): 1.96 (95% CI: 1.28–3.01).

provenance.notes: Predictors/points/rows from CFF export; complete meta from paper; verify vs live form.
